name: Aral Sea Basin
waterStressRatio: > 90% — virtually all inflow consumed before reaching lake
depletionRate: Critical — Aral Sea lost 90% of volume since 1960
description: The Aral Sea basin represents the world's most dramatic water catastrophe. Once the fourth-largest lake on Earth, the Aral Sea lost 90% of its volume as the Syr Darya and Amu Darya were diverted for cotton irrigation. The exposed seabed — the Aralkum desert — generates toxic salt and pesticide dust storms affecting 5 million people. Kazakhstan's North Aral Sea has partially recovered thanks to the Kok-Aral Dam, but the South Aral is effectively gone.
